name: Content Uploader
description: Drag-drop media upload component with file preview, progress tracking, and multi-file support. Handles images, videos, audio, and documents.

standard.description: Multi-file upload with preview grid and progress tracking

slots.dropzone: Drag-drop target area with visual feedback

single.description: Single file upload with large preview area

visual_brief: Large dashed-border drop zone with a cloud upload icon centered above 'Drag and drop files here or click to browse' text. Active drag-over state highlights the zone with a primary color border. Below the drop zone, an uploaded files list shows each file as a row with thumbnail preview, filename, file size, a progress bar during upload, and a remove button. The progress bar fills with primary color. The single preset shows one file at a time with a larger preview. Audio preset includes waveform visualization for audio files.

responsive.mobile: Drop zone reduces in height. File list shows smaller thumbnails. Upload uses native file picker on tap. Progress bars are full-width.
responsive.tablet: Standard drop zone size. File list shows medium thumbnails with inline progress.
responsive.desktop: Large drop zone with generous padding. File list shows large thumbnails and detailed file info.

layout_hints.dropzone_priority: The primary affordance is the upload target itself. Supporting file previews and action rows should reinforce confidence in what has been selected, not compete with the dropzone.
layout_hints.progress_clarity: Upload state, file identity, and error or retry status should stay tightly grouped for each file so the user can resolve issues without scanning the whole surface.
layout_hints.single_vs_multi: Single-file flows should emphasize preview and replacement, while multi-file flows should emphasize queue state and per-file progress.

motion.micro: Drop targets may brighten on drag-over, and upload rows may sharpen or pulse subtly while active.
motion.transitions: File entry, progress updates, and completion states should feel confidence-building and calm, especially during longer uploads.

keyboard: Browse, remove, retry, and upload actions should remain reachable in a straightforward order after the dropzone.
announcements: Selected files, upload progress, completion, and error states should be exposed semantically so the workflow remains understandable without drag feedback alone.
